productive_behavior: The controller rejected an invalid first reconciliation artifact and misleading zero-formatted p-values.
The repair regenerated a reader-facing table with bounded precision and p < 0.001 formatting.
The repair generated a legible two-panel figure whose pixel text and OCR both said ANCOVA.

scientific_defect.reason: The p-value corresponds to approximately 38 degrees of freedom, while the reported degrees of freedom and confidence interval use approximately 76; the tuple cannot come from one Student-t test.

critic_defects: The article critic demanded at least four table decimals even though deterministic validation found excessive reader-table precision.
The display critic simultaneously said the raster showed ANCOVA and blocked a purported MANGA typo; controller OCR and manual pixel inspection contained ANCOVA only.
orchestration_defect: After the repaired Python call, no new run event was emitted for more than 15 minutes; cooperative cancellation stopped the obsolete run and preserved partial artifacts.
disposition: Retained as a failed scientific-quality fixture; not counted as a passing release evaluation.
implemented_repairs: Generated JSON t/df/p tuples are recomputed against Student-t tails and fail closed when impossible.
The Qwen execution prompt requires inferential values to originate from one test object and documents compatible plotting APIs.
Critic requests that reverse an excessive-precision deterministic finding are discarded while the raw critic record remains available.

A typography blocker is discarded only when Gemma contradicts its own visual transcription and OCR from the exact same display contains the proposed correction but not the alleged typo; raw testimony remains preserved.
